nameMeanings.name: Strickland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): habitational name from Great and Little Strickland (Westmorland) or Strickland Ketel and Strickland Roger (also Westmorland). The placenames derive from Old English stīrc ‘stirk, young bullock or heifer’ + land ‘land’.

nameMeanings.name: Christina
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Simplified form of Latin Christiana, feminine form of Christianus ( see Christian ), or a Latinized form of Middle English Christin ‘Christian’ (Old English christen, from Latin).

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: Christina Strickland was born in 1232, in Strickland Ketel, Westmorland, England. She had at least 3 sons and 5 daughters with Thomas De Hastings III. She died in 1286, in England, at the age of 54.

ko.lifeSummary: Christina Strickland(은)는 1232년 영국 웨스트모를랜드 스트릭랜드 케텔에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Thomas De Hastings III(와)과 적어도 세 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1286년 영국에서 54세의 나이로 사망했다.
